definitions.definition: An action or operation which can produce a number of outcomes is called an experiment.
definitions.example: Tossing a coin is an experiment as it can produce two outcomes: heads or tails.

definitions.definition: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment is called the sample space.
definitions.example: When a die is rolled, the sample space is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}.

definitions.definition: Any subset of the sample space is called an event.
definitions.example: When a die is rolled, an event could be getting an even number.

definitions.definition: An event containing only one outcome is called a simple event.
definitions.example: Getting 4 when a die is rolled is a simple event.

definitions.definition: An event containing more than one outcome is called a compound event.
definitions.example: Getting an even number when a die is rolled is a compound event.

definitions.definition: If two or more events cannot occur together, then they are said to be mutually exclusive events.
definitions.example: Getting an even number and getting an odd number when a die is rolled are mutually exclusive events.

definitions.definition: If the sum of probabilities of all possible outcomes of an experiment is 1, then the events are called collectively exhaustive events.
definitions.example: When a die is rolled, the events of getting 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6 are collectively exhaustive events.

definitions.definition: The chance or likelihood of occurrence of an event is called the probability of the event.
definitions.example: Probability of getting a head when a coin is tossed is 1/2.

definitions.definition: An experiment in which all possible outcomes are known and the exact outcome cannot be predicted in advance is called a random experiment.
definitions.example: Tossing a coin is a random experiment.

definitions.definition: A variable which takes a random value is called a random variable.
definitions.example: The number of heads obtained when a coin is tossed 3 times is a random variable.

definitions.definition: A function which gives the probability of occurrence of each value of a random variable is called a probability distribution.
definitions.example: The probability distribution of the number of heads obtained when a coin is tossed 3 times.

definitions.definition: A random variable which takes a finite or countably infinite number of values is called a discrete random variable.
definitions.example: The number of heads obtained when a coin is tossed 3 times is a discrete random variable.

definitions.definition: A random variable which takes an uncountable number of values is called a continuous random variable.
definitions.example: The height of a person in a class is a continuous random variable.

definitions.definition: The function which gives the probability distribution of a continuous random variable is called the probability density function (pdf).
definitions.example: The probability density function of the height of a person in a class.

definitions.definition: The function which gives the probability that the random variable takes a value less than or equal to a given value is called the cumulative distribution function (cdf).
definitions.example: The cumulative distribution function of the number of heads obtained when a coin is tossed 3 times.

definitions.definition: Two events are said to be independent if the occurrence or non-occurrence of one event does not affect the probability of the occurrence of the other event.
definitions.example: Tossing a coin and rolling a die are independent events.

definitions.definition: Two events are said to be dependent if the occurrence or non-occurrence of one event affects the probability of the occurrence of the other event.
definitions.example: Drawing a card from a pack of cards and then drawing a card from the remaining cards are dependent events.

definitions.definition: A sequence of independent trials in which each trial has only two possible outcomes is called a Bernoulli trial.
definitions.example: Tossing a coin 3 times is a Bernoulli trial.
